product_overview: CiscoAutoFlash is a Windows desktop application that provides a serial-first workflow for Cisco 2960-X maintenance, supporting real-hardware smoke runs and a demo/replay harness for pre-hardware validation.
core_goals: Enable reliable serial/USB-first scan, install, and verification flows for Cisco 2960-X during pre-hardware smoke runs
Provide replayable demo scenarios and GUI self-tests that exercise full workflows without hardware
Produce complete session artifacts (logs, transcripts, reports, manifests, bundles) for operator audit and troubleshooting
Keep hidden backend SSH/SCP transport code present for future integration while not exposing SSH-first UX in this phase
Maintain pre-hardware stability and avoid changes that destabilize current Windows field packaging

features.name: Transport Discovery & Connection
features.description: Detect available serial/USB transports and establish a session to a target device so subsequent workflows can run over that transport.
user_flows: GET /api/transport/list -> 200 with ["COM3","COM4","USB0"] -> POST /api/transport/connect with {"port":"COM3","type":"serial"} -> 200 with {"session_id":"s1","status":"connected"} -> GET /api/scan?session_id=s1 -> 200 with {"target":"2960-X","state":"bootloader"}

GET /api/transport/list -> 200 with ["COM3","COM4"] -> POST /api/transport/connect with {"port":"COM99","type":"serial"} -> 404 with {"error":"port_not_found"} -> POST /api/transport/connect with {"port":"COM3","type":"serial"} -> 409 with {"error":"port_busy"}

features.name: Stage 1 — Reboot and Config Dialog Handling
features.description: Perform the initial reboot and answer interactive configuration dialogs presented by the 2960-X to reach an installable state.
user_flows: POST /api/session/command with {"session_id":"s1","command":"reload"} -> 200 with {"output":"Reloading..."} -> POST /api/stage1/config with {"session_id":"s1","answers":{"vlan_prompt":"no"}} -> 200 with {"status":"applied","next":"stage2"}

POST /api/session/command with {"session_id":"s1","command":"reload"} -> 200 with {"output":"Reloading..."} -> POST /api/stage1/config with {"session_id":"s1","answers":{}} -> 400 with {"error":"missing_required_field","field":"vlan_prompt"} -> GET /api/session/logs?session_id=s1 -> 200 with {"logs":["config dialog timed out"]}

features.name: Stage 2 — Install Flow and Timeout/Error Handling
features.description: Stage and transfer the IOS image, monitor installation job progress, and handle timeouts or transfer errors robustly.
user_flows: POST /api/stage2/install with {"session_id":"s1","image":"c2960x-universalk9-mz.150-2.SE.bin"} -> 202 with {"job_id":"j1"} -> GET /api/jobs/j1/status -> 200 with {"status":"completed","checksum":"abcd1234"} -> POST /api/stage2/verify_checksum with {"job_id":"j1","expected":"abcd1234"} -> 200 with {"result":"ok"}

POST /api/stage2/install with {"session_id":"s1","image":"missing.bin"} -> 400 with {"error":"image_not_found"} -> POST /api/stage2/install with {"session_id":"s1","image":"c2960x-universalk9-mz.150-2.SE.bin"} -> 202 with {"job_id":"j2"} -> GET /api/jobs/j2/status -> 200 with {"status":"timeout","detail":"transfer_timeout"} -> POST /api/stage2/abort with {"job_id":"j2"} -> 200 with {"status":"aborted"}

features.name: Stage 3 — Verification and Reporting
features.description: Run post-install verification checks and produce operator-visible reports indicating pass/fail and relevant evidence.
user_flows: POST /api/stage3/verify with {"session_id":"s1"} -> 200 with {"status":"pass","report_id":"r1"} -> GET /api/reports/r1 -> 200 with {"report":{"summary":"verification passed","checks":[...]}}

POST /api/stage3/verify with {"session_id":"s1"} -> 422 with {"errors":["verification_mismatch"]} -> POST /api/stage3/retry with {"session_id":"s1","retry_mode":"soft"} -> 200 with {"status":"retry_started","retry_id":"r_retry_1"} -> GET /api/reports?session_id=s1 -> 200 with {"reports":[{"report_id":"r1","status":"failed"}]}

features.name: Session Artifacts and Export
features.description: Collect and export session artifacts (logs, transcripts, manifests, reports) into an exportable bundle stored under the runtime/demo paths for operator handoff and debugging.
user_flows: POST /api/artifacts/collect with {"session_id":"s1","types":["log","transcript","report","manifest"]} -> 201 with {"artifact_ids":["a1","a2","a3","a4"]} -> POST /api/artifacts/create_bundle with {"artifact_ids":["a1","a2","a3","a4"],"target":"%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\session_bundles"} -> 201 with {"bundle_path":"%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\session_bundles\\s1_bundle.zip"} -> GET /api/artifacts/download?path=%LOCALAPPDATA%\\CiscoAutoFlash\\session_bundles\\s1_bundle.zip -> 200 with binary

features.name: Demo Mode and Replay Scenarios
features.description: Run canned replay scenarios (scan, stage1, stage2, stage3, full-install) and a lightweight demo mode so operators can validate GUI behavior and the orchestration logic without hardware.
user_flows: POST /api/demo/replay/start with {"scenario":"full_install","mode":"demo"} -> 200 with {"run_id":"d1","status":"running"} -> GET /api/demo/replay/d1/status -> 200 with {"status":"completed","result":"pass"} -> GET /api/demo/replay/d1/artifacts -> 200 with {"artifacts":["log","transcript","manifest"]}

POST /api/demo/replay/start with {"scenario":"unknown_scenario"} -> 404 with {"error":"scenario_not_found"} -> GET /api/demo/replay/list -> 200 with {"available_scenarios":["scan","stage1","stage2","stage3","full_install"]} -> POST /api/demo/replay/start with {"scenario":"scan","mode":"demo"} -> 200 with {"run_id":"d2"}

project.name: ciscoautoflash
project.description: CiscoAutoFlash desktop tool for Cisco 2960-X serial and USB workflows.

modules.role: Russian-first desktop dashboard and operator workflow UI
Workflow orchestration, reporting, session artifacts, transports
Demo and replay harness for pre-hardware validation
Canned scan, stage1, stage2, stage3 and full-install fixtures
Hardware smoke runbooks and readiness documentation

features: Serial-first operator workflow for Cisco 2960-X over COM and USB
Dashboard with preflight, operator status, target selection, and diagnostics tabs
Demo mode for click-smoke without hardware
Replay-backed scenarios for scan, stage1, stage2, and stage3 verification
Session artifacts including logs, transcripts, reports, manifests, and exportable bundles
Hidden backend-only SSH and SCP transport support prepared for later integration
Portable Windows field bundle already built for real-hardware smoke use

quality_state.current_goal: pre-hardware stabilization and demo GUI self-test
notes: Real-hardware scope is serial/USB only for the current phase
SSH transport exists but is not exposed in the operator UI yet
